target.name: Netrasemi
target.founder: Jyothis Indirabhai, Deepa Geetha, Sreejith Varma

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Netrasemi is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Jyothis Indirabhai, Deepa Geetha, and Sreejith Varma. The company has raised ₹107 Cr (~$13M) to date. The valuation exercise references a comparables universe of 8 private peers and 3 public semiconductor companies (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Peer-median valuation is the applicable method here. Four same-stage, same-sector private comparables—Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M)—establish a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 25.3x across 3 tickers) cannot be applied absent Netrasemi's revenue disclosure, which is typical for private-stage companies.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.3B (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median point estimate of $1.5B. This implies a post-money valuation approximately 115x the capital deployed to date. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer set—Etched at $5.0B represents an outlier outcome—and the absence of revenue or unit economics data, which would refine the range materially. The wide peer dispersion ($500M–$5.0B) also signals heterogeneity within Series A semiconductor investment, suggesting Netrasemi's positioning within that cohort remains a critical driver of final valuation.
